user.title: Smart Schedule
user.description:
Bundles a target device, three override modes (Off / On / Auto) and a weekly schedule in one helper.

💡 **Linked schedule** — leave empty and a native Schedule helper with the same name will be created automatically (e.g. 'Livingroom Plan' → `schedule.livingroom_plan`). Or pick an existing schedule here to link to that one instead.

In your Lovelace cards you only need to reference this Smart Schedule entity — the linked schedule is exposed via its attributes.

data.name: Name
data.linked_schedule: Linked schedule helper (leave empty to auto-create)
data.restore_auto_on_boundary: Resume Auto on next schedule boundary

data_description.target_entity: The switch, light or input_boolean that this helper automatically turns on/off when in Auto mode.
data_description.linked_schedule: Optional. Leave empty to auto-create a new schedule helper with the same name. Pick an existing schedule entity to link to that one instead.
data_description.mode_off_name: Display name for the Off mode (force device off).
data_description.mode_off_icon: Icon shown while in Off mode.
data_description.mode_on_name: Display name for the On mode (force device on).
data_description.mode_on_icon: Icon shown while in On mode.
data_description.mode_auto_name: Display name for the Auto mode (device follows the linked schedule).
data_description.mode_auto_icon: Icon shown while in Auto mode.
data_description.restore_auto_on_boundary: If enabled, a manual On/Off override is automatically lifted at the next schedule state change.

error.duplicate_mode_names: The three mode names must be different.

abort.already_configured: This configuration is already set up.

init.title: Smart Schedule options
init.description: Adjust the helper settings. The linked schedule shown below is the one auto-created when this helper was set up — open that helper under Settings → Helpers to edit its weekly plan. You can also re-link to a different schedule here if needed.

data.name: Name
data.restore_auto_on_boundary: Resume Auto on next schedule boundary

data_description.linked_schedule: The schedule entity whose on/off state this Smart Schedule follows in Auto mode. By default it's the one auto-created at setup time — change only if you want to follow a different schedule.

error.duplicate_mode_names: The three mode names must be different.
